Jae Woong Hwang

Vehicle Systems Validation Engineer

Hardware-near vehicle systems engineer with production experience in commercial-vehicle ADAS, embedded control, and real-vehicle validation.

I turn messy field behavior into replayable tools, structured evidence, and clear engineering stories across vehicle dynamics, control logic, calibration workflows, and validation environments.

South Korea
jwhwang91@gmail.com
+82-10-5775-1863
Portfolio Source

Target Roles

Vehicle Systems Validation Commercial-Vehicle ADAS Embedded Control Software Hardware-Near Software Real-Vehicle Testing MILS / HILS / Dynamometer Replay-Based Debugging Validation Toolchains Vehicle Dynamics / Control Sensor Fusion / Road-Curvature Estimation CANape / VN1640 / XCP / CAN-FD MATLAB / Simulink Python / C++ Tooling AI-Transition Platforms

Professional Summary

  • Vehicle systems validation engineer with hands-on production experience across commercial-vehicle ADAS behavior logic, embedded control software, and real-vehicle validation.
  • Hardware-near engineering background spanning KAIST real-hardware motion control, K2 tracked-vehicle TCU software productization, and HMC commercial-vehicle ADAS mass-production development.
  • Turns messy field behavior into structured evidence by connecting real logs, vehicle signals, ECU logic, calibration workflows, and replay-based validation tools.
  • Builds practical validation toolchains under real engineering constraints, including production BEV target-selection debugging and resource-constrained AI-transition platforms for ADAS module owners.

Core Competencies

Control: Vehicle dynamics / Commercial-vehicle ADAS controls / Embedded control software / SCC/L2 ADAS behavior logic / Front-target deceleration behavior / Target-selection robustness / Road-curvature estimation / Sensor fusion

Validation: Vehicle systems validation / MILS / HILS / Dynamometer / Real-vehicle validation / Replay-based debugging / Calibration/debugging / Release-readiness support

Software: Python / C/C++ / MATLAB/Simulink / CANape/VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / ONNX / Validation toolchains / AI-transition platforms

Professional Experience

Researcher - KAIST Master's Research Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) | 2015.03 - 2017.08
Technical Case Study

Real-hardware control foundation: designed and validated motion-stabilization control for turret hardware under moving-platform disturbance.

  • Built a foundation in dynamics, control, estimation, sensor noise handling, and hardware validation through real turret motion-control research.
  • Designed control logic to stabilize turret motion under artificial base-motion disturbance, connecting model-based controller design with physical hardware behavior.
  • Developed the control and estimation background later reused in vehicle dynamics, road-curvature estimation, and sensor-fusion work.

Detail: experience/kaist-masters/index.html

Researcher - TCU Application Software Agency for Defense Development (ADD), Ground Technology Research Institute | 2017.09 - 2021.03
Technical Case Study

Full V-cycle embedded control experience: developed K2 tracked-vehicle TCU software from control logic and plant modeling to HILS, dynamometer, and field validation.

  • Developed application software for the K2 tracked-vehicle transmission control unit, working close to physical drivetrain behavior, actuator constraints, and vehicle-level validation.
  • Experienced full V-cycle validation across MILS, HILS, powerpack dynamometer, and actual vehicle field testing.
  • Worked on sensorless shift control, plant modeling, calibration, parameter correction, and reliability-test support for heavy-duty transmission software productization.

Detail: experience/add-k2-tcu/index.html

Commercial Vehicle ADAS Controls & Validation Engineer Hyundai Motor Company (HMC) | 2021.03 - Present
Technical Case Study

Production commercial-vehicle ADAS work across SCC/L2 behavior logic, road-curvature fusion, target-selection robustness, and real-vehicle validation.

  • Developed and validated selected production ADAS behavior logic for commercial trucks, tractors, buses, and fuel-cell platform variants.
  • Owned independent contributions including SCC emergency-stop behavior and EKF-based RoadRadius fusion using steering-angle and yaw-rate information under commercial-vehicle sensing constraints.
  • Supported SCC tuning, FOC tuning, state-transition modification, regression testing, vehicle-level debugging, and release-readiness activities across MILS, HILS, dynamometer, and real-vehicle workflows.
  • Built and used replay-based validation tooling to analyze false target-selection behavior with real logs and production TOS/ODP logic, reducing repeated real-bus validation loops for rare bus-lane scenarios.
  • Designed resource-constrained AI-transition toolchains that reused existing CANape/VN1640/MF4 workflows to help ADAS module owners experiment with learned models before dedicated AI validation infrastructure was available.

Detail: experience/hmc-adas/index.html

Selected Technical Projects

Public-safe technical case studies for validation tooling, AI-assisted implementation, and commercial-vehicle ADAS debugging workflows.

Turning a Legacy Calibration Setup into an ADAS AI Validation Loop Python / C++ / CANape / VN1640 / XCP over CAN-FD / ONNX
Technical Case Study GitHub

Resource-constrained AI-transition platform for testing learned-model behavior on real vehicle signals using the team's existing calibration setup.

Objective: Build a minimum viable real-vehicle AI validation loop for a commercial-vehicle ADAS team that had limited access to Ethernet-based validation equipment and high-end AI compute platforms. Instead of waiting for full infrastructure, the project reused the available CANape/VN1640/laptop workflow to make learned-model experimentation approachable for ADAS module owners.

Impact: Created a practical bridge from conventional calibration workflows to AI-assisted validation by connecting learned models, real vehicle signals, and XCP-over-CAN-FD communication under existing field-test constraints. The project is positioned as a proactive AI-transition prototype rather than a fully deployed production tool.

Python C++ XCP CAN-FD ONNX CANape/VN1640

Detail: toolchains/e2e-xcp-bypass/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/realtime-vehicle-ai-validation-studio

ADAS Log-to-AI Workbench Python / MF4 / PyTorch / ONNX / MATLAB Export
Technical Case Study GitHub

CANape/MF4-to-AI workflow for turning real vehicle measurement logs into AI-ready datasets, training runs, and exportable model artifacts.

Objective: Help ADAS module owners reuse familiar CANape/MF4 measurement data for AI experimentation without requiring a full ML platform from day one. The workbench structures preprocessing, trimming, normalization, model-script execution, evaluation, and export into a repeatable learning workflow.

Impact: Defined a practical transition path from conventional vehicle-log analysis to AI model experimentation. It reduces the entry barrier for module owners to study learned-model behavior, compare preprocessing choices, and generate artifacts for later validation workflows, while remaining clearly positioned as an AI-transition support prototype.

Python MF4 PyTorch ONNX MATLAB Export

Detail: toolchains/timeseries-ai-training/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/adas-log-to-ai-workbench

BEV Target Selection Validation Studio MATLAB Script / Simulink / Base Workspace / ADAS Visualization
Technical Case Study GitHub

Production-development replay studio for explaining and reducing false target-selection behavior using real logs and production TOS/ODP logic.

Objective: Reproduce rare commercial-vehicle target-selection edge cases in a repeatable MATLAB/Simulink workflow instead of relying on repeated real-bus road tests after every TOS/ODP logic or parameter change.

Impact: Actively used during commercial-vehicle mass-production development to replay real logs through production TOS/ODP logic, investigate false detections, and prepare logic or calibration improvements before final vehicle confirmation. For rare bus-only-lane scenarios, it helped reduce repeated real-bus validation trips from about 10 to around 5, with each trip typically requiring 4-5 hours.

MATLAB Simulink ADAS BEV TOS/ODP

Detail: toolchains/tos-odp-bev-simulator/index.html

GitHub: https://github.com/jwhwang91/bev-target-selection-validation-studio
